Title:Microsoft Windows 11 STIG SCAP Benchmark - NIWC Enhanced with Manual Questions
Description:This Security Technical Implementation Guide is published as a tool to improve the security of Department of Defense (DOD) information systems. The requirements are derived from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) 800-53 and related documents. Comments or proposed revisions to this document should be sent via email to the following address: disa.stig_spt@mail.mil.

Results: High Severity (CAT I)

    Automated Checks

    • V-253259 - Windows 11 information systems must use BitLocker to encrypt all disks to protect the confidentiality and integrity of all information at rest. - Fail
    • V-253260 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication. - Fail
    • V-253283 - Data Execution Prevention (DEP) must be configured to at least OptOut. - Fail
    • V-253284 - Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) must be enabled. - Fail
    • V-253382 - Solicited Remote Assistance must not be allowed. - Fail
    • V-253386 - Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices. - Fail
    • V-253387 - The default autorun behavior must be configured to prevent autorun commands. - Fail
    • V-253388 - Autoplay must be disabled for all drives. - Fail
    • V-253411 - The Windows Installer feature "Always install with elevated privileges" must be disabled. - Fail
    • V-253416 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Basic authentication. - Fail
    • V-253418 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not use Basic authentication. - Fail
    • V-253454 - Anonymous enumeration of shares must be restricted. - Fail
    • V-253462 - The LanMan authentication level must be set to send NTLMv2 response only, and to refuse LM and NTLM. - Fail

      • V-253436 - The built-in guest account must be renamed. - Fail

      • V-253445 - The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon. - Fail

      • V-253451 - The Windows SMB server must be configured to always perform SMB packet signing. - Fail

      • V-253465 - The system must be configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based servers. - Fail
      • V-253466 - The system must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing. - Fail

      • V-253483 - The "Back up files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group. - Fail

        Manual Checks

        Detailed Results: High Severity (CAT I)

          V-253259 - Windows 11 information systems must use BitLocker to encrypt all disks to protect the confidentiality and integrity of all information at rest.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253259r958870_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000030
          Identities:CCI-002475 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-28 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-28 (1))
          Description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running.
          Fix Text:Enable full disk encryption on all information systems (including SIPRNet) using BitLocker.

          BitLocker, included in Windows, can be enabled in the Control Panel under "BitLocker Drive Encryption" as well as other management tools.

          Note: An alternate encryption application may be used in lieu of BitLocker providing it is configured for full disk encryption and satisfies the pre-boot authentication requirements (WN11-00-000031 and WN11-00-000032).
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253259
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000030 - Windows 11 information systems must use BitLocker to encrypt all disks to protect the confidentiality and integrity of all information at rest.
          Description:If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (BitLocker must be enabled on all fixed drives.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25325900 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:BitLocker must be enabled on all fixed drives.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25325900 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:BitLocker status
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2\security\microsoftvolumeencryption'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT protectionstatus FROM win32_encryptablevolume'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25325900 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:BitLocker status is Protected
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all protectionstatus must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2\security\microsoftvolumeencryption'
          • wql equals 'SELECT protectionstatus FROM win32_encryptablevolume'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • protectionstatus = '0'
          Result Analysis:protectionstatus = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

          V-253260 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253260r958872_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000031
          Identities:CCI-002476 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-28 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-28 (1))
          Description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> BitLocker Drive Encryption >> Operating System Drives "Require additional authentication at startup" to "Enabled" with "Configure TPM Startup PIN:" set to "Require startup PIN with TPM" or with "Configure TPM startup key and PIN:" set to "Require startup key and PIN with TPM".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253260
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000031 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication.
          Description:If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                    • false ('Configure TPM startup PIN' is set to 'Require startup PIN with TPM'.)
                    • false ('Configure TPM startup key and PIN' is set to 'Require startup key and PIN with TPM'.)
                  • true ('Allow network unlock at startup' is Enabled.) (negated)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                    • false ('Configure TPM startup PIN' is set to 'Allow startup PIN with TPM'.)
                    • false ('Configure TPM startup key and PIN' is set to 'Allow startup key and PIN with TPM'.)
                  • false ('Allow network unlock at startup' is Enabled.)
              • false ('Require additional authentication at startup' is Enabled.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25326002 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Configure TPM startup PIN' is set to 'Require startup PIN with TPM'.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25326002 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'UseTPMPIN'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE\UseTPMPIN'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25326003 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Configure TPM startup key and PIN' is set to 'Require startup key and PIN with TPM'.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25326003 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'UseTPMKeyPIN'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE\UseTPMKeyPIN'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25326000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Allow network unlock at startup' is Enabled.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25326000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'OSManageNKP'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment:

          V-253283 - Data Execution Prevention (DEP) must be configured to at least OptOut.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253283r958928_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000145
          Identities:CCI-002824 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-16; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-16)
          Description: Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Data Execution Prevention (DEP) prevents harmful code from running in protected memory locations reserved for Windows and other programs.
          Fix Text:Configure DEP to at least OptOut.

          Note: Suspend BitLocker before making changes to the DEP configuration.

          Open a command prompt (cmd.exe) or PowerShell with elevated privileges (Run as administrator).
          Enter "BCDEDIT /set {current} nx OptOut". (If using PowerShell "{current}" must be enclosed in quotes.)
          "AlwaysOn", a more restrictive selection, is also valid but does not allow applications that do not function properly to be opted out of DEP.

          Opted out exceptions can be configured in the "System Properties".

          Open "System" in Control Panel.
          Select "Advanced system settings".
          Click "Settings" in the "Performance" section.
          Select the "Data Execution Prevention" tab.
          Applications that are opted out are configured in the window below the selection "Turn on DEP for all programs and services except those I select:".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253283
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000145 - Data Execution Prevention (DEP) must be configured to at least OptOut.
          Description:Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Data Execution Prevention (DEP) prevents harmful code from running in protected memory locations reserved for Windows and other programs.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Data Execution Prevention (DEP) is set to 'OptOut' or 'AlwaysOn')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328300 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Data Execution Prevention (DEP) is set to 'OptOut' or 'AlwaysOn'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328300 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT dataexecutionprevention_supportpolicy FROM win32_operatingsystem'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328300 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Always On
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all dataexecutionprevention_supportpolicy must be equal to '1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328301 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Opt Out
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all dataexecutionprevention_supportpolicy must be equal to '3'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT dataexecutionprevention_supportpolicy FROM win32_operatingsystem'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • dataexecutionprevention_supportpolicy = '2'
          Result Analysis:dataexecutionprevention_supportpolicy = '2' failed test of 'equals' compared to '3'
          dataexecutionprevention_supportpolicy = '2' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

          V-253284 - Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253284r958928_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000150
          Identities:CCI-002824 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-16; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-16)
          Description: Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) blocks exploits that use the Structured Exception Handling overwrite technique, a common buffer overflow attack.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Enable Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP)" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253284
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000150 - Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) must be enabled.
          Description:Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) blocks exploits that use the Structured Exception Handling overwrite technique, a common buffer overflow attack.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (SEHOP is turned on and properly configured)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:SEHOP is turned on and properly configured
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\kernel'
          • name must be equal to 'DisableExceptionChainValidation'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\kernel\DisableExceptionChainValidation'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253382 - Solicited Remote Assistance must not be allowed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253382r958524_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000155
          Identities:CCI-001090 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-4.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-4)
          Description: Remote assistance allows another user to view or take control of the local session of a user. Solicited assistance is help that is specifically requested by the local user. This may allow unauthorized parties access to the resources on the computer.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Remote Assistance >> "Configure Solicited Remote Assistance" to "Disabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:

          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Configure Solicited Remote Assistance' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Configure Solicited Remote Assistance' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
          • name must be equal to 'fAllowToGetHelp'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\fAllowToGetHelp'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253386 - Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253386r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000180
          Identities:CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. This setting will disable autoplay for non-volume devices (such as Media Transfer Protocol (MTP) devices).
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253386
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000180 - Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices.
          Description:Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. This setting will disable autoplay for non-volume devices (such as Media Transfer Protocol (MTP) devices).
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'
          • name must be equal to 'NoAutoplayfornonVolume'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\NoAutoplayfornonVolume'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253387r958804_rule

          Version:WN11-CC-000185

          V-253388 - Autoplay must be disabled for all drives.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253388r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000190
          Identities:CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. By default, autoplay is disabled on removable drives, such as the floppy disk drive (but not the CD-ROM drive) and on network drives. If this policy is enabled, autoplay can be disabled on all drives.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Turn off AutoPlay" to "Enabled:All Drives".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253388
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000190 - Autoplay must be disabled for all drives.
          Description:Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. By default, autoplay is disabled on removable drives, such as the floppy disk drive (but not the CD-ROM drive) and on network drives. If this policy is enabled, autoplay can be disabled on all drives.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Turn off AutoPlay' is set to 'Enabled:All Drives')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Turn off AutoPlay' is set to 'Enabled:All Drives'
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • name must be equal to 'NoDriveTypeAutoRun'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25338800 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 255
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '255'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\NoDriveTypeAutoRun'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253411 - The Windows Installer feature "Always install with elevated privileges" must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253411r1016439_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000315
          Identities:CCI-003980 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-11 (2))
          CCI-001812 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-11 (2))
          CCI-001812 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-11 (2))
          Description: Standard user accounts must not be granted elevated privileges. Enabling Windows Installer to elevate privileges when installing applications can allow malicious persons and applications to gain full control of a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Installer >> "Always install with elevated privileges" to "Disabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:

          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Always install with elevated privileges' is set to 'Disabled')

          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer'
          • name must be equal to 'AlwaysInstallElevated'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253418r958510_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000345
          Identities:CCI-000877 (NIST SP 800-53: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53A: MA-4.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 c)
          Description: Basic authentication uses plain text passwords that could be used to compromise a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Service >> "Allow Basic authentication" to "Disabled".

          Severity Override Guidance: The AO can allow the severity override if they have reviewed the overall protection. This would only be allowed temporarily for implementation as documented and approved.
          ....
          Allowing Basic authentication to be used for the sole creation of Office 365 DoD tenants.
          ....
          A documented mechanism and or script that can disable Basic authentication once administration completes.
          ....
          Use of a Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) and adherence to the Clean Source principle for administration.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253418
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000345 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not use Basic authentication.
          Description:Basic authentication uses plain text passwords that could be used to compromise a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Allow Basic authentication' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Allow Basic authentication' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Service'
          • name must be equal to 'AllowBasic'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Service\AllowBasic'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253454 - Anonymous enumeration of shares must be restricted.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253454r958524_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000150
          Identities:CCI-001090 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-4.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-4)
          Description: Allowing anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all account names and enumerate all shared resources can provide a map of potential points to attack the system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253454
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000150 - Anonymous enumeration of shares must be restricted.
          Description:Allowing anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all account names and enumerate all shared resources can provide a map of potential points to attack the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name must be equal to 'restrictanonymous'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'restrictanonymous'
          • last_write_time equals '134248737310000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\restrictanonymous\value = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

          V-253462 - The LanMan authentication level must be set to send NTLMv2 response only, and to refuse LM and NTLM.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253462r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000205
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The Kerberos v5 authentication protocol is the default for authentication of users who are logging on to domain accounts. NTLM, which is less secure, is retained in later Windows versions for compatibility with clients and servers that are running earlier versions of Windows or applications that still use it. It is also used to authenticate logons to stand-alone computers that are running later versions.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: LAN Manager authentication level" to "Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253462
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000205 - The LanMan authentication level must be set to send NTLMv2 response only, and to refuse LM and NTLM.
          Description:The Kerberos v5 authentication protocol is the default for authentication of users who are logging on to domain accounts. NTLM, which is less secure, is retained in later Windows versions for compatibility with clients and servers that are running earlier versions of Windows or applications that still use it. It is also used to authenticate logons to stand-alone computers that are running later versions.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Network security: LAN Manager authentication level' is set to 'Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM' (Level 5))
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Network security: LAN Manager authentication level' is set to 'Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM' (Level 5)
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name must be equal to 'LmCompatibilityLevel'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25346200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 5
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '5'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\LmCompatibilityLevel'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

        Detailed Results: Medium Severity (CAT II)

          V-253254 - Domain-joined systems must use Windows 11 Enterprise Edition 64-bit version.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253254r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000005
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Features such as Credential Guard use virtualization-based security to protect information that could be used in credential theft attacks if compromised. There are a number of system requirements that must be met in order for Credential Guard to be configured and enabled properly. Virtualization-based security and Credential Guard are only available with Windows 11 Enterprise 64-bit version.
          Fix Text:Use Windows 11 Enterprise 64-bit version for domain-joined systems.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253254
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000005 - Domain-joined systems must use Windows 11 Enterprise Edition 64-bit version.
          Description:Features such as Credential Guard use virtualization-based security to protect information that could be used in credential theft attacks if compromised. There are a number of system requirements that must be met in order for Credential Guard to be configured and enabled properly. Virtualization-based security and Credential Guard are only available with Windows 11 Enterprise 64-bit version.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (EditionID is Enterprise)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25325400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:EditionID is Enterprise
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25325400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'EditionId'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25325400 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to 'Enterprise'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion'
          • name equals 'EditionId'
          • last_write_time equals '134248738390000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals 'Professional'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\EditionId\value = 'Professional' failed test of 'equals' compared to 'Enterprise'

          V-253261 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN with a minimum length of six digits for pre-boot authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253261r958504_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000032
          Identities:CCI-000804 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-8.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-8)
          Description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives. Increasing the pin length requires a greater number of guesses for an attacker.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> BitLocker Drive Encryption >> Operating System Drives "Configure minimum PIN length for startup" to "Enabled" with "Minimum characters:" set to "6" or greater.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253261
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000032 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN with a minimum length of six digits for pre-boot authentication.
          Description:If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives. Increasing the pin length requires a greater number of guesses for an attacker.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (BitLocker must be 6 digits or more.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25326100 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:BitLocker must be 6 digits or more.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25326100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check for BitLocker MinimumPIN
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MinimumPIN'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25326100 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be greater than or equal to '6'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE\MinimumPIN'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253273 - Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253273r1016425_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000090
          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          Description: Passwords that do not expire increase exposure with a greater probability of being discovered or cracked.
          Fix Text:Configure all passwords to expire.
          Run "Computer Management".
          Navigate to System Tools >> Local Users and Groups >> Users.
          Double-click each active account.
          Ensure "Password never expires" is not checked on all active accounts.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:381
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000076-GPOS-00044, SV-253273r1016425
          Description:Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:381 (cmdlet_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:381 (cmdlet_object)
          Object Comment:Enabled Local Accounts with PasswordExpires=False should not exist
          Object Requirements:
          • module_name must be equal to 'Microsoft.Powershell.Management'
          • verb must be equal to 'Get'
          • noun must be equal to 'CimInstance'
          • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
            • class must be equal to 'Win32_Useraccount'
            • filter must be equal to 'PasswordExpires=False and LocalAccount=True and Disabled=false'
          • for 'select' the following must be true:
            • unique must be equal to 'Name,PasswordExpires,Disabled,LocalAccount'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ exists ]
          • module_name equals 'Microsoft.PowerShell.Management'
          • module_id equals '{eefcb906-b326-4e99-9f54-8b4bb6ef3c6d}'
          • module_version equals '3.1.0.0'
          • verb equals 'Get'
          • noun equals 'CimInstance'
          • collected 'parameters' result:
            • class = 'Win32_Useraccount'
            • filter = 'PasswordExpires=False and LocalAccount=True and Disabled=false'
          • collected 'select' result:
            • unique = 'Name,PasswordExpires,Disabled,LocalAccount'
          • collected 'value' result:
            • name = 'admin'
            • passwordexpires = 'False'
            • disabled = 'False'
            • localaccount = 'True'
          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'none_exists' and 1 collected item exists, so result is false

          V-253285 - The Windows PowerShell 2.0 feature must be disabled on the system.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253285r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000155
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Windows PowerShell 5.0 added advanced logging features which can provide additional detail when malware has been run on a system. Disabling the Windows PowerShell 2.0 mitigates against a downgrade attack that evades the Windows PowerShell 5.0 script block logging feature.
          Fix Text:Disable "Windows PowerShell 2.0" on the system.

          Run "Windows PowerShell" with elevated privileges (run as administrator).
          Enter the following:
          Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName MicrosoftWindowsPowerShellV2Root

          This command must disable both "MicrosoftWindowsPowerShellV2Root" and "MicrosoftWindowsPowerShellV2" which correspond to "Windows PowerShell 2.0" and "Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine" respectively in "Turn Windows features on or off".

          Alternately:
          Search for "Features".
          Select "Turn Windows features on or off".
          De-select "Windows PowerShell 2.0".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253285
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000155 - The Windows PowerShell 2.0 feature must be disabled on the system.
          Description:Windows PowerShell 5.0 added advanced logging features which can provide additional detail when malware has been run on a system. Disabling the Windows PowerShell 2.0 mitigates against a downgrade attack that evades the Windows PowerShell 5.0 script block logging feature.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Windows PowerShell 2.0 optional feature is disabled)
              • false (Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine optional feature is disabled)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328500 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Windows PowerShell 2.0 optional feature is disabled
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328500 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:Windows PowerShell 2.0 Win32_OptionalFeature object
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'MicrosoftWindowsPowerShellV2Root''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328500 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Check Windows PowerShell 2.0 Win32_OptionalFeature object InstallState is equal to 2
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • at least one installstate must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'MicrosoftWindowsPowerShellV2Root''
          Result Analysis:. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          .
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328501 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine optional feature is disabled
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328501 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine Win32_OptionalFeature object
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'MicrosoftWindowsPowerShellV2''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328500 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Check Windows PowerShell 2.0 Win32_OptionalFeature object InstallState is equal to 2
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • at least one installstate must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT installstate FROM win32_optionalfeature WHERE name = 'MicrosoftWindowsPowerShellV2''
          Result Analysis:. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253289 - The Secondary Logon service must be disabled on Windows 11.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253289r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000175
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: The Secondary Logon service provides a means for entering alternate credentials, typically used to run commands with elevated privileges. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft.
          Fix Text:Configure the "Secondary Logon" service "Startup Type" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253289
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000175 - The Secondary Logon service must be disabled on Windows 11.
          Description:The Secondary Logon service provides a means for entering alternate credentials, typically used to run commands with elevated privileges. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The Secondary Logon service Startup Type is Disabled or the service is uninstalled)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328900 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The Secondary Logon service Startup Type is Disabled or the service is uninstalled
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328900 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:Secondary Logon Service Startup Type
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='seclogon''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328900 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Service Startup Type is Disabled
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all startmode must be equal to 'Disabled'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='seclogon''
          • collected 'result' result:
            • startmode = 'Manual'
          Result Analysis:startmode = 'Manual' failed test of 'equals' compared to 'Disabled'

          V-253297 - Windows 11 account lockout duration must be configured to 15 minutes or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253297r958736_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000005
          Identities:CCI-002238 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 b)
          Description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the amount of time that an account will remain locked after the specified number of failed logon attempts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy >> "Account lockout duration" to "15" minutes or greater.

          A value of "0" is also acceptable, requiring an administrator to unlock the account.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253297
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AC-000005 - Windows 11 account lockout duration must be configured to 15 minutes or greater.
          Description:The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the amount of time that an account will remain locked after the specified number of failed logon attempts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false ('Account lockout duration' is set to '0')
                • false ('Account lockout duration' is set to '15 minutes' or greater)
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329700 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Account lockout duration' is set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329700 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Duration equals -1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', lockout_duration must be less than or equal to '0'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329701 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Duration equals 4294967295
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', lockout_duration must be equal to '4294967295'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '600'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '600'
          • lockout_threshold equals '10'
          Result Analysis:lockout_duration = '600' failed test of 'equals' compared to '4294967295'
          lockout_duration = '600' failed test of 'less than or equal' compared to '0'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329701 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Account lockout duration' is set to '15 minutes' or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329702 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Duration greater than or equals 900
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', lockout_duration must be greater than or equal to '900'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '600'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '600'
          • lockout_threshold equals '10'

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

          State Requirements:

          V-253298 - The number of allowed bad logon attempts must be configured to three or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253298r958388_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000010
          Identities:CCI-000044 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 a)
          Description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. The higher this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system. The number of bad logon attempts must be reasonably small to minimize the possibility of a successful password attack, while allowing for honest errors made during a normal user logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy >> "Account lockout threshold" to "3" or less invalid logon attempts (excluding "0" which is unacceptable).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253298
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AC-000010 - The number of allowed bad logon attempts must be configured to three or less.
          Description:The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. The higher this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system. The number of bad logon attempts must be reasonably small to minimize the possibility of a successful password attack, while allowing for honest errors made during a normal user logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Account lockout threshold' is set to '3' or less)
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329800 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is set to '3' or less
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329800 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is less than or equal to 3
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', lockout_threshold must be less than or equal to '3'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '600'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '600'
          • lockout_threshold equals '10'
          Result Analysis:lockout_threshold = '10' failed test of 'less than or equal' compared to '3'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is greater than 0
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', lockout_threshold must be greater than '0'

          V-253299 - The period of time before the bad logon counter is reset must be configured to 15 minutes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253299r958388_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000015
          Identities:CCI-000044 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 a)
          Description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the period of time that must pass after failed logon attempts before the counter is reset to 0. The smaller this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy >> "Reset account lockout counter after" to "15" minutes.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253299
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AC-000015 - The period of time before the bad logon counter is reset must be configured to 15 minutes.
          Description:The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the period of time that must pass after failed logon attempts before the counter is reset to 0. The smaller this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Reset account lockout counter after' is set to at least the required value)
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329900 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Reset account lockout counter after' is set to at least the required value
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329900 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Observation Window is greater than or equal to variable
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', lockout_observation_window must be greater than or equal to '900'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '600'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '600'
          • lockout_threshold equals '10'
          Result Analysis:lockout_observation_window = '600' failed test of 'greater than or equal' compared to '900'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is greater than 0
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', lockout_threshold must be greater than '0'

          V-253300 - The password history must be configured to 24 passwords remembered.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253300r1000103_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000020
          Identities:CCI-004061 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (b))
          Description: A system is more vulnerable to unauthorized access when system users recycle the same password several times without being required to change a password to a unique password on a regularly scheduled basis. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes. The default value is 24 for Windows domain systems. DOD has decided this is the appropriate value for all Windows systems.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Enforce password history" to "24" passwords remembered.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253300
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AC-000020 - The password history must be configured to 24 passwords remembered.
          Description:A system is more vulnerable to unauthorized access when system users recycle the same password several times without being required to change a password to a unique password on a regularly scheduled basis. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes. The default value is 24 for Windows domain systems. DoD has decided this is the appropriate value for all Windows systems.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Enforce password history' is set to '24' passwords or more)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330000 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Enforce password history' is set to '24' passwords or more
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330000 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Password History Length is greater than or equal to variable
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', password_hist_len must be greater than or equal to '24'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • max_passwd_age equals '3628800'
          • min_passwd_age equals '0'
          • min_passwd_len equals '0'
          • password_hist_len equals '0'
          • password_complexity equals '0'
          • reversible_encryption equals '0'
          • anonymous_name_lookup equals '0'
          Result Analysis:password_hist_len = '0' failed test of 'greater than or equal' compared to '24'

          V-253302 - The minimum password age must be configured to at least 1 day.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253302r1016428_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000030
          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000198 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          CCI-000198 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          Description: Permitting passwords to be changed in immediate succession within the same day allows users to cycle passwords through their history database. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Minimum Password Age" to at least "1" day.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471

          Definition Result:false

          Description:Permitting passwords to be changed in immediate succession within the same day allows users to cycle passwords through their history database. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Minimum Password Age' is set to at least '1' day)
          Tests:

          Result:false
          Title:'Minimum Password Age' is set to at least '1' day
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330200 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', min_passwd_age must be greater than or equal to '86400'

          V-253303 - Passwords must, at a minimum, be 14 characters.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253303r1016429_rule

          Description: Information systems not protected with strong password schemes (including passwords of minimum length) provide the opportunity for anyone to crack the password, thus gaining access to the system and compromising the device, information, or the local network.

          V-253304 - The built-in Microsoft password complexity filter must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253304r1016430_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000040
          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))

          Description: The use of complex passwords increases their strength against guessing and brute-force attacks. This setting configures the system to verify that newly created passwords conform to the Windows password complexity policy.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Password must meet complexity requirements" to "Enabled".
