          V-253306 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253306r991570_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000005
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Logon >> "Audit Credential Validation" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253306
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000005 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Failure')
              • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Credential Validation' is set to 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', credential_validation must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • distribution_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_management_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • dpapi_activity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • process_creation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • process_termination equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • rpc_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253307 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253307r991570_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000010
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Logon >> "Audit Credential Validation" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253307
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000010 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success')
              • false ('Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Credential Validation' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          • Collect any available items.

          V-253309 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253309r958566_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000035
          Identities:CCI-001314 (NIST SP 800-53: SI-11 c; NIST SP 800-53A: SI-11.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-11 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-11 b)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit User Account Management" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253309
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000035 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Failure')
              • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:

          V-253311 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253311r1016432_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000045
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-003938 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-5 (1) (b))
          CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
          CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Plug and Play activity records events related to the successful connection of external devices.
          Fix Text:Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit PNP Activity" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:1141
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000365-GPOS-00152, SV-253311r1016432
          Description:The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled)
            • false (Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:10000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:10000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Secure Boot State Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • name must be equal to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:41 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:1141 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:1141 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieves a auditeventpolicysubcategories_object.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:1141 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:pnp_activity subcategory must be Success.
          State Requirements:
          • for check = 'all', pnp_activity, all of the following must be true:
            • pnp_activity must match the pattern 'AUDIT_(SUCCESS|SUCCESS_FAILURE)'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253312 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253312r1016433_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000050
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)

          CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
          CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Process creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit Process Creation" with "Success" selected.

          Weight:10.0

          V-253313 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253313r991578_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000054
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Account Lockout events can be used to identify potentially malicious logon attempts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Account Lockout" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253313
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000054 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Account Lockout events can be used to identify potentially malicious logon attempts.
          Class:compliance

            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))

              • false ('Audit Account Lockout' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')

          V-253314 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253314r991570_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000060
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Group Membership records information related to the group membership of a user's logon token.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Group Membership" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:1201
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SV-253314r991570
          Description:The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled)
            • false (Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:10000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:10000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Secure Boot State Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • name must be equal to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:41 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:1201 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:1141 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieves a auditeventpolicysubcategories_object.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:1201 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:group_membership subcategory must be Success.
          State Requirements:
          • for check = 'all', group_membership, all of the following must be true:
            • group_membership must match the pattern 'AUDIT_(SUCCESS|SUCCESS_FAILURE)'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          • process_termination equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • rpc_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • directory_service_changes equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • account_lockout equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • ipsec_extended_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_quick_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logoff equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • network_policy_server equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • other_logon_logoff_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • special_logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • logon_claims equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_generated equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • audit_detailedtracking_tokenrightadjusted equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          Result Analysis:group_membership = 'AUDIT_NONE' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to AUDIT_(SUCCESS|SUCCESS_FAILURE)

          V-253319 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253319r991572_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000081
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File Share" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253319
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000081 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit File Share' is set to 'Failure')
              • false ('Audit File Share' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')

          V-253321 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253321r991572_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000083
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Other Object Access Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253321
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000083 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success')
              • false ('Audit Other Object Access Events' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')

          V-253323 - The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253323r991583_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000085
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Removable Storage" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:1381
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000474-GPOS-00219, SV-253323r991583
          Description:The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled)
            • false (Removable Storage - Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:10000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:10000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Secure Boot State Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • name must be equal to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:41 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:1381 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Removable Storage - Failure
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:1141 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieves a auditeventpolicysubcategories_object.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:1381 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:removable storage subcategory must be Failure.
          State Requirements:
          • for check = 'all', removable_storage, all of the following must be true:
            • removable_storage must match the pattern 'AUDIT_(FAILURE|SUCCESS_FAILURE)'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253324 - The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253324r991583_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000090
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Removable Storage" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:1401
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000474-GPOS-00219, SV-253324r991583
          Description:The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage successes.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled)
            • false (Removable Storage - Success)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:10000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:10000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Secure Boot State Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • name must be equal to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:41 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:1401 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Removable Storage - Success
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:1141 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieves a auditeventpolicysubcategories_object.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:1401 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:removable storage subcategory must be Success.
          State Requirements:
          • for check = 'all', removable_storage, all of the following must be true:
            • removable_storage must match the pattern 'AUDIT_(SUCCESS|SUCCESS_FAILURE)'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253327 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authorization Policy Change successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253327r991572_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000107
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as create a token object.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Authorization Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253327
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000107 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authorization Policy Change successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as create a token object.
          Class:compliance

              • false (Audit - Audit Policy Change - Success and Failure)

          V-253328 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253328r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000110
          Identities:CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253328
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000110 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
          Class:compliance

          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))

              • false ('Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure')

          V-253329 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253329r991575_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000115
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253329
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000115 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)

          V-253330 - The system must be configured to audit System - IPsec Driver failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253330r991586_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000120
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          IPsec Driver records events related to the IPsec Driver such as dropped packets.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit IPsec Driver" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253330
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000120 - The system must be configured to audit System - IPsec Driver failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          IPsec Driver records events related to the IPsec Driver such as dropped packets.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Audit - IPSec Driver - Failure only)
              • false (Audit - IPSec Driver - Success and Failure)

          V-253334 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security System Extension successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253334r991575_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000150
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Security System Extension" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471

Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.

          V-253337 - The Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253337r958752_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000500
          Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Fix Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Application >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "32768" or greater.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253337
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000500 - The Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.
          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be greater than or equal to '32768'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253338 - The Security event log size must be configured to 1024000 KB or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253338r958752_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000505
          Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Security >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "1024000" or greater.

          If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this must be documented with the ISSO.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA

          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The Security event log size is configured to 1024000 KB or greater)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The Security event log size is configured to 1024000 KB or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333800 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be greater than or equal to '1024000'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253343 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253343r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000550
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Policy Change Events contains events about EFS Data Recovery Agent policy changes, changes in Windows Filtering Platform filter, status on Security policy settings updates for local Group Policy settings, Central Access Policy changes, and detailed troubleshooting events for Cryptographic Next Generation (CNG) operations.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change>> "Audit Other Policy Change Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253343
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000550 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Policy Change Events contains events about EFS Data Recovery Agent policy changes, changes in Windows Filtering Platform filter, status on Security policy settings updates for local Group Policy settings, Central Access Policy changes, and detailed troubleshooting events for Cryptographic Next Generation (CNG) operations.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Other Policy Change Events' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Other Policy Change Events' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', other_policy_change_events must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334301 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', other_policy_change_events must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253345 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253345r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000560
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253345
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000560 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure

          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          V-253346 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253346r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000565
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253346
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000565 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334600 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', other_logon_logoff_events must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

          V-253347 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Detailed File Share Failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253347r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000570
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Detailed File Share allows the user to audit attempts to access files and folders on a shared folder.
          The Detailed File Share setting logs an event every time a file or folder is accessed, whereas the File Share setting only records one event for any connection established between a client and file share. Detailed File Share audit events include detailed information about the permissions or other criteria used to grant or deny access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Detailed File Share" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253347
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000570 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Detailed File Share Failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Detailed File Share allows the user to audit attempts to access files and folders on a shared folder.
          The Detailed File Share setting logs an event every time a file or folder is accessed, whereas the File Share setting only records one event for any connection established between a client and file share. Detailed File Share audit events include detailed information about the permissions or other criteria used to grant or deny access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Detailed File Share' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Detailed File Share' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', detailed_file_share must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334701 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', detailed_file_share must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-253348 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253348r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000575
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253348

          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).

          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253349
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000580 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' and 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' and 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334901 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • distribution_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_management_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • dpapi_activity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • process_creation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • process_termination equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • rpc_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_access equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_changes equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • account_lockout equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • ipsec_extended_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_quick_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logoff equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • network_policy_server equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • other_logon_logoff_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • special_logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • logon_claims equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_generated equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • certification_services equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • detailed_file_share equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • file_share equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • file_system equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • central_access_policy_staging equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • authentication_policy_change equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          • authorization_policy_change equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • filtering_platform_policy_change equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_policy_change_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • non_sensitive_privilege_use equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_privilege_use_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • sensitive_privilege_use equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_driver equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • security_system_extension equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
