          V-253351 - Windows 11 must cover or disable the built-in or attached camera when not in use.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253351r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000007
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: It is detrimental for operating systems to provide, or install by default, functionality exceeding requirements or mission objectives. These unnecessary capabilities or services are often overlooked and therefore may remain unsecured. They increase the risk to the platform by providing additional attack vectors.

          Failing to disconnect from collaborative computing devices (i.e. cameras) can result in subsequent compromises of organizational information. Providing easy methods to physically disconnect from such devices after a collaborative computing session helps to ensure that participants actually carry out the disconnect activity without having to go through complex and tedious procedures.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000095-GPOS-00049, SRG-OS-000370-GPOS-00155
          Fix Text:If the camera is not disconnected or covered, the following registry entry is required.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          RegistryPath\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CapabilityAccessManager\ConsentStore\webcam

          Value Name: Deny
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:1941
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000095-GPOS-00049, SV-253351r958478
          Description:Windows 11 must cover or disable the built-in or attached camera when not in use.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Camera access from the lock screen must be disabled.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:1941 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Camera access from the lock screen must be disabled.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:1941 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Prevent enabling lock screen camera
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'
          • name must be equal to 'NoLockScreenCamera'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:41 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenCamera'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253352 - The display of slide shows on the lock screen must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253352r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000010
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged on user.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Control Panel >> Personalization >> "Prevent enabling lock screen slide show" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253352
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000010 - The display of slide shows on the lock screen must be disabled.
          Description:Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged on user.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Registry object for HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'
          • name must be equal to 'NoLockScreenSlideshow'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253353 - IPv6 source routing must be configured to highest protection.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253353r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000020
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Configuring the system to disable IPv6 source routing protects against spoofing.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)" to "Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253353
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000020 - IPv6 source routing must be configured to highest protection.
          Description:Configuring the system to disable IPv6 source routing protects against spoofing.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335300 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting IPv6) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters'
          • name must be equal to 'DisableIPSourceRouting'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '2'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters\DisableIPSourceRouting'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253354r991589_rule

          V-253357 - Local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253357r958518_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000037
          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
          Description: A compromised local administrator account can provide means for an attacker to move laterally between domain systems.

          With User Account Control enabled, filtering the privileged token for built-in administrator accounts will prevent the elevated privileges of these accounts from being used over the network.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Apply UAC restrictions to local accounts on network logons" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253357
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000037 - Local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain systems.
          Description:A compromised local administrator account can provide means for an attacker to move laterally between domain systems.

          With User Account Control enabled, filtering the privileged token for built-in administrator accounts will prevent the elevated privileges of these accounts from being used over the network.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Local admin accounts filtered token policy is enabled)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Local admin accounts filtered token policy is enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • name must be equal to 'LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25335700 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253358 - WDigest Authentication must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253358r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000038
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows 11. This setting ensures this is enforced.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "WDigest Authentication (disabling may require KB2871997)" to "Disabled".

          The patch referenced in the policy title is not required for Windows 11.

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253358
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000038 - WDigest Authentication must be disabled.
          Description:When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows 11. This setting ensures this is enforced.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (WDigest Authentication is disabled)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:WDigest Authentication is disabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest'
          • name must be equal to 'UseLogonCredential'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest\UseLogonCredential'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253359 - Run as different user must be removed from context menus.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253359r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000039
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: The "Run as different user" selection from context menus allows the use of credentials other than the currently logged on user. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft. Removing this option from context menus helps prevent this from occurring.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Remove "Run as Different User" from context menus" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253359
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000039 - Run as different user must be removed from context menus.
          Description:The "Run as different user" selection from context menus allows the use of credentials other than the currently logged on user. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft. Removing this option from context menus helps prevent this from occurring.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (batfile))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (cmdfile))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (exefile))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (mscfile))
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (batfile)
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Classes\batfile\shell\runasuser'
          • name must be equal to 'SuppressionPolicy'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25335900 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 4096
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '4096'

          V-253360 - Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253360r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000040
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Lanman Workstation >> "Enable insecure guest logons" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253360
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000040 - Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.
          Description:Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation'
          • name must be equal to 'AllowInsecureGuestAuth'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation\AllowInsecureGuestAuth'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253361 - Internet connection sharing must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253361r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000044
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Internet connection sharing makes it possible for an existing internet connection, such as through wireless, to be shared and used by other systems essentially creating a mobile hotspot. This exposes the system sharing the connection to others with potentially malicious purpose.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Network Connections >> "Prohibit use of Internet Connection Sharing on your DNS domain network" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253361
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000044 - Internet connection sharing must be disabled.
          Description:Internet connection sharing makes it possible for an existing internet connection, such as through wireless, to be shared and used by other systems essentially creating a mobile hotspot. This exposes the system sharing the connection to others with potentially malicious purpose.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Prohibit use of Internet Connection Sharing on your DNS domain network' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336100 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Prohibit use of Internet Connection Sharing on your DNS domain network' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Network Connections'
          • name must be equal to 'NC_ShowSharedAccessUI'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Network Connections\NC_ShowSharedAccessUI'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253362 - Hardened UNC Paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253362r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000050
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Additional security requirements are applied to Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths specified in Hardened UNC paths before allowing access them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Network Provider >> "Hardened UNC Paths" to "Enabled" with at least the following configured in "Hardened UNC Paths:" (click the "Show" button to display).

          Value Name: \\*\SYSVOL
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1

          Value Name: \\*\NETLOGON
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253362
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000050 - Hardened UNC Paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares.
          Description:Additional security requirements are applied to Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths specified in Hardened UNC paths before allowing access them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL)
              • false (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\SYSVOL
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • name must be equal to '\\*\SYSVOL'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireIntegrity=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireIntegrity!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths\\\*\SYSVOL'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336201 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336201 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\NETLOGON
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • name must be equal to '\\*\NETLOGON'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)

          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)

          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths\\\*\NETLOGON'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253365 - Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253365r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000060
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Multiple network connections can provide additional attack vectors to a system and must be limited. When connected to a domain, communication must go through the domain connection.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Windows Connection Manager >> "Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253365
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000060 - Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked.
          Description:Multiple network connections can provide additional attack vectors to a system and must be limited. When connected to a domain, communication must go through the domain connection.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336500 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\'
          • name must be equal to 'fBlockNonDomain'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\\fBlockNonDomain'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253366 - Wi-Fi Sense must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253366r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000065
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Wi-Fi Sense automatically connects the system to known hotspots and networks that contacts have shared. It also allows the sharing of the system's known networks to contacts. Automatically connecting to hotspots and shared networks can expose a system to unsecured or potentially malicious systems.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> WLAN Service >> WLAN Settings>> "Allow Windows to automatically connect to suggested open hotspots, to networks shared by contacts, and to hotspots offering paid services" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253366
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000065 - Wi-Fi Sense must be disabled.
          Description:Wi-Fi Sense automatically connects the system to known hotspots and networks that contacts have shared. It also allows the sharing of the system's known networks to contacts. Automatically connecting to hotspots and shared networks can expose a system to unsecured or potentially malicious systems.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Verifies 'Allow Windows to automatically connect to suggested open hotspots, to networks shared by contacts, and to hotspots offering paid service' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Verifies 'Allow Windows to automatically connect to suggested open hotspots, to networks shared by contacts, and to hotspots offering paid service' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:AutoConnectAllowedOEM
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WcmSvc\wifinetworkmanager\config'
          • name must be equal to 'AutoConnectAllowedOEM'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WcmSvc\wifinetworkmanager\config\AutoConnectAllowedOEM'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253367 - Command line data must be included in process creation events.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253367r958422_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000066
          Identities:CCI-000135 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Audit Process Creation >> "Include command line in process creation events" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253367
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000066 - Command line data must be included in process creation events.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit'
          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit\ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253368 - Windows 11 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253368r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000068
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Credentials Delegation >> "Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253368
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000068 - Windows 11 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.
          Description:An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:

          V-253372 - Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253372r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000085
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The default behavior is for Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy is to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad"). By being launched first by the kernel, ELAM ( Early Launch Antimalware) is ensured to be launched before any third-party software, and is therefore able to detect malware in the boot process and prevent it from initializing.
          Fix Text:Ensure that Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy is set to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad").

          To correct this, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Early Launch Antimalware >> "Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy" to "Enabled with "Good, unknown and bad but critical" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253372
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000085 - Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers.
          Description:The default behavior is for Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy is to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad"). By being launched first by the kernel, ELAM ( Early Launch Antimalware) is ensured to be launched before any third-party software, and is therefore able to detect malware in the boot process and prevent it from initializing.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\EarlyLaunch'
          • name must be equal to 'DriverLoadPolicy'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000003 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '3'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25337200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 8
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '8'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\EarlyLaunch\DriverLoadPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253373 - Group Policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253373r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000090
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures that the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Group Policy >> "Configure registry policy processing" to "Enabled" and select the option "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253373
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000090 - Group Policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.
          Description:Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures that the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337300 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337300 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}'
          • name must be equal to 'NoGPOListChanges'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}\NoGPOListChanges'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253374 - Downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be prevented.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253374r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000100
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> "Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253374
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000100 - Downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be prevented.
          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
          • name must be equal to 'DisableWebPnPDownload'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\DisableWebPnPDownload'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253375 - Web publishing and online ordering wizards must be prevented from downloading a list of providers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253375r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000105
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents Windows from downloading a list of providers for the Web publishing and online ordering wizards.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> "Turn off Internet download for Web publishing and online ordering wizards" to "Enabled".

Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents Windows from downloading a list of providers for the Web publishing and online ordering wizards.
          Class:compliance

          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • name must be equal to 'NoWebServices'

          V-253376 - Printing over HTTP must be prevented.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253376r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000110
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the internet.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> "Turn off printing over HTTP" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253376
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000110 - Printing over HTTP must be prevented.
          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the internet.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
          • name must be equal to 'DisableHTTPPrinting'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\DisableHTTPPrinting'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253378r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail

          • name must be equal to 'DontDisplayNetworkSelectionUI'

          V-253379 - Local users on domain-joined computers must not be enumerated.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253379r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000130
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: The username is one part of logon credentials that could be used to gain access to a system. Preventing the enumeration of users limits this information to authorized personnel.
          Fix Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Logon >> "Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253379
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000130 - Local users on domain-joined computers must not be enumerated.
          Description:The username is one part of logon credentials that could be used to gain access to a system. Preventing the enumeration of users limits this information to authorized personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
          • name must be equal to 'EnumerateLocalUsers'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\EnumerateLocalUsers'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253380 - Users must be prompted for a password on resume from sleep (on battery).

          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
          Description: Authentication must always be required when accessing a system. This setting ensures the user is prompted for a password on resume from sleep (on battery).

          V-253383 - Unauthenticated RPC clients must be restricted from connecting to the RPC server.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253383r971545_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000165
          Identities:CCI-001967 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-3 (1))
          Description: Configuring RPC to restrict unauthenticated RPC clients from connecting to the RPC server will prevent anonymous connections.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Remote Procedure Call >> "Restrict Unauthenticated RPC clients" to "Enabled" and "Authenticated".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11

          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Rpc'
          • name must be equal to 'RestrictRemoteClients'

          V-253389 - Enhanced anti-spoofing for facial recognition must be enabled on Windows 11.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253389r991589_rule

          V-253391 - Administrator accounts must not be enumerated during elevation.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253391r958518_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000200
          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
          Description: Enumeration of administrator accounts when elevating can provide part of the logon information to an unauthorized user. This setting configures the system to always require users to type in a username and password to elevate a running application.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Credential User Interface >> "Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471

          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation' is set to 'Disabled')

          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339100 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI'
          • name must be equal to 'EnumerateAdministrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
