          V-253491 - The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253491r958472_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000070
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny access to this computer from the network" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins group
          Domain Admins group
          Local account (see Note below)

          All Systems:
          Guests group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253491
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000070 - The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins)
                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins)
                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account)
                  • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • true (The system is a Windows Domain Member Workstation)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation)
              • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349101 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349102 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349103 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000007 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000200 (wmi57_test)
          Result:true
          Title:The system is a Windows Domain Member Workstation
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000200 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Domain Member Workstation
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all domainrole must be equal to '1'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000100 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000100 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Standalone Workstation
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all domainrole must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
          • collected 'result' result:
            • domainrole = '1'
          Result Analysis:domainrole = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349100 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for Guests
          Result Analysis:sedenynetworklogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

          V-253492 - The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253492r958472_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000075
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a batch job" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.
          Fix Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a batch job" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admin Group
          Domain Admin Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253492
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000075 - The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a batch job" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Domain Admin groups.)
              • false (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Enterprise Admin groups.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349200 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Domain Admin groups.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenybatchLogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349201 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Enterprise Admin groups.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenybatchLogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253493 - The "Deny log on as a service" user right on Windows 11 domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253493r958472_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000080
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a service" right defines accounts that are denied log on as a service.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a DoS.
          Fix Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a service" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins Group
          Domain Admins Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:

          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000080 - The "Deny log on as a service" user right on Windows 11 domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a service" right defines accounts that are denied log on as a service.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a DoS.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Deny log on as a service - Domain Admins)
              • false (Deny log on as a service - Enterprise Admins)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349300 (accesstoken_test)

          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349300 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on as a service
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyservicelogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349301 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false

          V-253494 - The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253494r958472_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000085
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on locally" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on locally" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins Group
          Domain Admins Group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          All Systems:
          Guests Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253494
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000085 - The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on locally" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (Deny log on locally - Domain Admins)
                  • false (Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins)
                • false (System is not a member of a domain)
              • false (Deny log on locally - Guests)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349402 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on locally - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on locally
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyinteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349403 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyinteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'

          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349401 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:System is not a member of a domain
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'

          V-253495 - The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows 11 workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253495r958472_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000090
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          CCI-002314 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

          If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the Everyone group must be assigned this right to prevent all access.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" to include the following:

          If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, assign the Everyone group this right to prevent all access.

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins group
          Domain Admins group
          Local account (see Note below)

          All Systems:
          Guests group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253495
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000090 - The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows 11 workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

          If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the Everyone group must be assigned this right to prevent all access.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                  • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins)
                    • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Enterprise Admins)
                    • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Local account)
                    • true (System is a Windows non-DC domain-joined system)
                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation or Server)
                • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Guests)
              • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Everyone)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349501 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349502 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Enterprise Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349503 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Local account
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000007 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]

          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349504 (wmi57_test)

          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000200 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Domain Member Workstation
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:

          V-253498 - The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253498r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000110
          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right allows a program to impersonate another user or account to run on their behalf. An attacker could potentially use this to elevate privileges.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Impersonate a client after authentication" to only include the following groups or accounts:

          Administrators
          LOCAL SERVICE
          NETWORK SERVICE
          SERVICE
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253498
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000110 - The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right allows a program to impersonate another user or account to run on their behalf. An attacker could potentially use this to elevate privileges.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (No groups or accounts are granted the 'Impersonate a client after authentication' user right.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349800 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:No groups or accounts are granted the 'Impersonate a client after authentication' user right.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000013 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators, Local Service, Network Service, Service
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'LOCAL SERVICE'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'NETWORK SERVICE'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'SERVICE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349800 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seimpersonateprivilege must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • seimpersonateprivilege equals '1' for: RESTRICTED SERVICES\PrintSpoolerService
          • seimpersonateprivilege equals '0' for Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, User Mode Hardware Operators, Authenticated Users, DefaultAccount, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Guests, Remote Desktop Users, PROXY, BATCH, Remote Management Users, ANONYMOUS LOGON, System Managed Accounts Group, Event Log Readers, CREATOR GROUP, Backup Operators, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, WO02\admin, OpenSSH Users, Device Owners, Performance Monitor Users, Hyper-V Administrators, Network Configuration Operators, IIS_IUSRS, INTERACTIVE, IUSR, Replicator, TERMINAL SERVER USER, NETWORK, Power Users, Administrator, SYSTEM, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, DIALUP, WDAGUtilityAccount, Distributed COM Users, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, Everyone, Access Control Assistance Operators, CREATOR OWNER
          Result Analysis:seimpersonateprivilege : RESTRICTED SERVICES\PrintSpoolerService = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          V-253505 - The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253505r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000160
          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to over-write more current data.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Restore files and directories" to only include the following groups or accounts:

          Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253505
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000160 - The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to over-write more current data.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Restore files and directories - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350500 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Restore files and directories - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350500 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Restore files and directories
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', serestoreprivilege must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • serestoreprivilege equals '1' for: Backup Operators
          • serestoreprivilege equals '0' for Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, LOCAL SERVICE, User Mode Hardware Operators, Authenticated Users, DefaultAccount, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, RESTRICTED SERVICES\PrintSpoolerService, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Guests, Remote Desktop Users, PROXY, BATCH, Remote Management Users, ANONYMOUS LOGON, System Managed Accounts Group, Event Log Readers, CREATOR GROUP, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, WO02\admin, OpenSSH Users, Device Owners, Performance Monitor Users, Hyper-V Administrators, SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, IIS_IUSRS, INTERACTIVE, IUSR, Replicator, TERMINAL SERVER USER, NETWORK, Power Users, Administrator, SYSTEM, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, DIALUP, NETWORK SERVICE, WDAGUtilityAccount, Distributed COM Users, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, Everyone, Access Control Assistance Operators, CREATOR OWNER
          Result Analysis:serestoreprivilege : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          V-257770 - Windows 11 must have command line process auditing events enabled for failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-257770r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000585
          Identities:CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: When this policy setting is enabled, the operating system generates audit events when a process fails to start and the name of the program or user that created it.

          These audit events can assist in understanding how a computer is being used and tracking user activity.
          Fix Text:Go to Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >>Security Settings>> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> Set "Audit Process Creation" to "Failure".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:257770
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000585 - Windows 11 must have command line process auditing events enabled.
          Description:When this policy setting is enabled, the operating system generates audit events when a process is created (starts) and the name of the program or user that created it.

          These audit events can help with understanding how a computer is being used and tracking user activity.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Audit - Process Creation - Failure only)
              • false (Audit - Process Creation - Success and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25777000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit - Process Creation - Failure only
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          V-268317 - Copilot in Windows must be disabled for Windows 11

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-268317r1016371_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000125
          Identities:CCI-000382 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 b)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Copilot >> "Turn off Windows Copilot" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:4791
          Definition Result:false
          Title: SRG-OS-000096-GPOS-00050, SV-268317r1016371
          Description:Copilot in Windows must be disabled for Windows 11
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Check all enabled user profiles HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot, verify TurnOffWindowsCopilot is REG_DWORD = 1)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:4791 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Check all enabled user profiles HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot, verify TurnOffWindowsCopilot is REG_DWORD = 1
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:4791 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment:DisableThirdPartySuggestions = 1
          Object Requirements:
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot'
          • name must be equal to 'TurnOffWindowsCopilot'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:4791 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-1326455503-2292008373-3661171645-1106'
          • username equals 'LAB\labtech'
          • account_type equals 'domain'
          • logged_on equals 'true'
          • enabled equals 'true'
          • date_modified equals '134248733370000000'
          • days_since_modified equals '1'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\labtech\ntuser.dat'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-1326455503-2292008373-3661171645-500'
          • username equals 'WO02\LAB\Administrator'
          • account_type equals 'domain'
          • logged_on equals 'false'
          • enabled equals 'true'
          • date_modified equals '134248733370000000'
          • days_since_modified equals '1'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\Administrator\ntuser.dat'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-906687523-2966097102-3869229598-1001'
          • username equals 'WO02\admin'
          • account_type equals 'local'
          • logged_on equals 'false'
          • enabled equals 'true'
          • date_modified equals '134248134300000000'
          • days_since_modified equals '1'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\admin\ntuser.dat'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found in user profile ntuser.dat file(s): 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot\TurnOffWindowsCopilot'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          For 'LAB\labtech': Registry Name Not found in user profile ntuser.dat file(s): 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot\TurnOffWindowsCopilot'.

          For 'WO02\admin': Registry Name Not found in user profile ntuser.dat file(s): 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot\TurnOffWindowsCopilot'.

        Detailed Results: Low Severity (CAT III)

          V-253355 - The system must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) generated routes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253355r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000030
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via shortest path first.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes" to "Disabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253355
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000030 - The system must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) generated routes.
          Description:Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via shortest path first.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335500 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters'
          • name must be equal to 'EnableICMPRedirect'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\EnableICMPRedirect'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253356 - The system must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253356r958902_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000035
          Identities:CCI-002385 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-5; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-5 a)
          Description: Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the servers WINS resolution capability.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow the computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253356
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000035 - The system must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.
          Description:Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the servers WINS resolution capability.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters'
          • name must be equal to 'NoNameReleaseOnDemand'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters\NoNameReleaseOnDemand'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253384 - The setting to allow Microsoft accounts to be optional for modern style apps must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253384r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000170
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Control of credentials and the system must be maintained within the enterprise. Enabling this setting allows enterprise credentials to be used with modern style apps that support this, instead of Microsoft accounts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> App Runtime >> "Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471

          Description:Control of credentials and the system must be maintained within the enterprise. Enabling this setting allows enterprise credentials to be used with modern style apps that support this, instead of Microsoft accounts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • name must be equal to 'MSAOptional'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\MSAOptional'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253385 - The Application Compatibility Program Inventory must be prevented from collecting data and sending the information to Microsoft.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253385r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000175
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting will prevent the Program Inventory from collecting data about a system and sending the information to Microsoft.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Application Compatibility >> "Turn off Inventory Collector" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253385
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000175 - The Application Compatibility Program Inventory must be prevented from collecting data and sending the information to Microsoft.
          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and uncontrolled updates to the system. This setting will prevent the Program Inventory from collecting data about a system and sending the information to Microsoft.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Turn off Inventory Collector' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338500 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Turn off Inventory Collector' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat'
          • name must be equal to 'DisableInventory'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat\DisableInventory'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253390 - Microsoft consumer experiences must be turned off.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253390r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000197
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Microsoft consumer experiences provides suggestions and notifications to users, which may include the installation of Windows Store apps. Organizations may control the execution of applications through other means such as allowlisting. Turning off Microsoft consumer experiences will help prevent the unwanted installation of suggested applications.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Cloud Content >> "Turn off Microsoft consumer experiences" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253390
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000197 - Microsoft consumer experiences must be turned off.
          Description:Microsoft consumer experiences provides suggestions and notifications to users, which may include the installation of Windows Store apps. Organizations may control the execution of applications through other means such as allowlisting. Turning off Microsoft consumer experiences will help prevent the unwanted installation of suggested applications.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Turn off Microsoft consumer experiences' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Turn off Microsoft consumer experiences' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent'

          V-253394 - Windows Update must not obtain updates from other PCs on the internet.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253394r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000206
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Windows 11 allows Windows Update to obtain updates from additional sources instead of Microsoft. In addition to Microsoft, updates can be obtained from and sent to PCs on the local network as well as on the Internet. This is part of the Windows Update trusted process, however to minimize outside exposure, obtaining updates from or sending to systems on the internet must be prevented.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Delivery Optimization >> "Download Mode" to "Enabled" with any option except "Internet" selected.

          Acceptable selections include:
          Bypass (100)
          Group (2)
          HTTP only (0)
          LAN (1)
          Simple (99)

          .
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253394
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000206 - Windows Update must not obtain updates from other PCs on the internet.
          Description:Windows 11 allows Windows Update to obtain updates from additional sources instead of Microsoft. In addition to Microsoft, updates can be obtained from and sent to PCs on the local network as well as on the Internet. This is part of the Windows Update trusted process, however to minimize outside exposure, obtaining updates from or sending to systems on the internet must be prevented.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation or Server)
                • false ('DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only or LAN' selected.)
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false ('DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only, LAN, Group, Simple or Bypass' selected.)
          Tests:

          Result:false

          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'

          V-253425 - Windows 11 must be configured to prevent users from receiving suggestions for third-party or additional applications.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253425r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000390
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Windows spotlight features may suggest apps and content from third-party software publishers in addition to Microsoft apps and content.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates. >> Windows Components >> Cloud Content >> "Do not suggest third-party content in Windows spotlight" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:3421
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000095-GPOS-00049, SV-253425r958478
          Description:Windows 11 must be configured to prevent users from receiving suggestions for third-party or additional applications.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Windows 11 must be configured to prevent users from receiving suggestions for third-party or additional applications.)

          Result:false
          Title:Windows 11 must be configured to prevent users from receiving suggestions for third-party or additional applications.

          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:3421 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment:DisableThirdPartySuggestions Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent'
          • name must be equal to 'DisableThirdPartySuggestions'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'

          V-253477 - Toast notifications to the lock screen must be turned off.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253477r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UC-000015
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Toast notifications that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged on user.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Start Menu and Taskbar >> Notifications >> "Turn off toast notifications on the lock screen" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:4461
          Definition Result:false
          Title:SRG-OS-000095-GPOS-00049, SV-253477r958478
          Description:Toast notifications to the lock screen must be turned off.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Toast notifications to the lock screen must be turned off.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:4461 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Toast notifications to the lock screen must be turned off.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:4461 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment:SaveZoneInformation Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications'
          • name must be equal to 'NoToastApplicationNotificationOnLockScreen'
          Exclude Items If:
          • enabled equals 'false'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:4461 (ntuser_state)
